hero.eyebrow: Open-source fractal art in your browser
hero.title: Explore the Infinite Beauty of Fractals
hero.subtitle: FractalPark is an MIT-licensed, browser-based fractal generator for everyone. Open a tab, explore 94 formulas, tune the colors, and export your own mathematical art.
hero.tags: Open Source MIT|Runs in Browser|94 Formulas|HD Export

empty.description: Star your favorite fractals to show them here.

seo.whatIsAnswer: FractalPark is a free, open-source (MIT-licensed) fractal generator that runs entirely in your web browser. It lets you explore 94 different fractal formulas — including the Mandelbrot set, Julia sets, Burning Ship, Newton, Phoenix, and more — color them with 7 different algorithms, and export high-resolution PNG images up to 4× zoom, all without installing any software.
seo.howToHeading: How to Generate Your First Fractal
howToSteps: Open the Explore page and pick a formula — the classic Mandelbrot set is a good start.
Drag on the canvas to pan and scroll to zoom into the fractal boundary where the detail lives.
Switch to the Coloring tab and try smooth iteration, orbit traps, or a custom gradient.
Open the Render tab to enable SSAA anti-aliasing and adaptive iteration depth.
Click Export to download a high-resolution PNG, or Share to copy a link that restores the exact view.
seo.familiesIntro: Every formula is implemented in GLSL with both Mandelbrot and Julia modes. Click any formula name in the explorer to load it with sensible defaults.

classic.name: Classic
classic.character: Iconic escape-time sets; cardioid and seahorse valley

burningShip.name: Burning Ship
burningShip.character: Asymmetric, ship-like silhouettes from abs() folding

newton.name: Newton
newton.character: Root-basin attractors; smooth basins with fractal boundaries

phoenix.name: Phoenix
phoenix.character: Feathered, flame-like filaments from a recurrence term

magnet.name: Magnet
magnet.examples: Magnet Type I, Magnet Type II
magnet.character: Dense cores with orbit chains; tight self-similarity

exotic.name: Exotic
exotic.character: Conjugate and hybrid variants with unusual symmetries

seo.featuresHeading: What You Can Do with FractalPark
features: 94 GLSL fractal formulas with Mandelbrot and Julia modes
7 coloring modes including smooth iteration, orbit traps, and custom gradients
Real-time WebGL rendering at 60 fps on any modern device
High-resolution PNG export up to 4× with SSAA anti-aliasing
Shareable URLs that restore the exact view and parameters
Custom formula editor with AST validation and live preview

free.a: Yes. FractalPark is completely free and open-source under the MIT license. You can use it for personal, educational, or commercial purposes without any cost.

chinese.a: Yes. FractalPark is fully bilingual — both the interface and the content are available in English and Simplified Chinese.

export.q: How do I export a fractal as a PNG?
export.a: Open the Render tab in the explorer, choose a scale (1×, 2×, or 4×), and click Export. FractalPark will render the image at high resolution with SSAA anti-aliasing and download it as a PNG file.

formulas.q: How many fractal formulas does FractalPark include?
formulas.a: FractalPark ships with 94 fractal formulas organized into 7 families: Classic, Burning Ship, Newton, Phoenix, Transcendental, Magnet, and Exotic. Each formula supports both Mandelbrot and Julia modes.

share.q: Can I share a fractal I created?
share.a: Yes. Every view in FractalPark has a unique URL that encodes the formula, position, zoom, coloring, and all other parameters. Click Share to copy the link — anyone who opens it will see the exact same fractal.

opensource.a: Yes. The entire codebase is MIT-licensed and available on GitHub.
opensource.linkText: View the FractalPark repository on GitHub

gallery.title: Gallery
gallery.description: Explore featured fractals or browse your saved collection.
gallery.fullscreenHint: Click anywhere or press Esc to exit

starterBrot.name: Starter Mandelbrot
starterBrot.description: The smallest escape-time formula, useful for learning the init / loop / bailout structure first.

parameterDrift.name: Parameter Drift
parameterDrift.description: Demonstrates p1 / p2 parameter slots and the difference between cabs and |z|.

orbitEcho.name: Orbit Echo
orbitEcho.description: Uses zPrev as feedback memory and pairs well with orbitEcho coloring for orbit envelopes.

fnSlotWeave.name: Fn Slot Weave
fnSlotWeave.description: Demonstrates fn1 / fn2 and parameter slots as a Fractint-compatible authoring template.

branchGarden.name: Branch Garden
branchGarden.description: Shows if / elseif / else branches mixed with trigonometric updates.

customLibrary.title: Custom Formulas
customLibrary.examplesDescription: Load a working example first, then reshape it into your own formula in the editor.

juliaC.label: Julia Parameter (c)
juliaC.pickHint: Tip: click a point on Mandelbrot to set c and switch to Julia.

controls.formulaNoParameters: This formula uses its built-in defaults and does not need extra controls.
controls.transformNoParameters: This transform uses its built-in defaults and does not need extra controls.

editor.title: Formula Editor
editor.loadFailedDescription: Unable to load the code editor. Please refresh the page.
editor.saveFailedDescription: Unable to save the current formula
editor.restoreSuccessDescription: The editor has been rolled back to the most recent successful source.
editor.defaultViewSetDescription: Saving will persist the current Explore view with this custom formula.
editor.glslError: GLSL compile error (mapped from formula source)
editor.glslErrorUnmapped: GLSL compile error (location cannot be mapped)

seo.heading: FractalPark Explorer — Free In-Browser Fractal Generator
seo.intro: FractalPark is a free, open-source (MIT) WebGL fractal generator that runs entirely in your browser. Explore 94 fractal formulas across 7 families — from the classic Mandelbrot and Julia sets to Burning Ship, Newton, Phoenix, and exotic transcendental attractors — and export high-resolution PNG images up to 4× without installing anything.
seo.howToHeading: How to Generate Your First Fractal
howToSteps: Pick a formula from the Formula tab — try the classic Mandelbrot set to start.
Drag on the canvas to pan, scroll to zoom into the boundary region where detail lives.
Switch to the Coloring tab and try smooth iteration, orbit traps, or a custom gradient.
Open the Render tab to enable anti-aliasing (SSAA) and adaptive iteration depth.
Click Export to download a high-resolution PNG, or Share to copy a link that restores the exact view.
seo.familiesIntro: Every formula is implemented in GLSL with both Mandelbrot and Julia modes. Click any formula name in the explorer to load it with sensible defaults.

home.title: FractalPark - Open Source Browser Fractal Generator
home.description: FractalPark is an open-source MIT-licensed fractal generator that runs in your browser. Explore 94 formulas, real-time WebGL rendering, custom palettes, and PNG export.
home.ogTitle: FractalPark - Open Source Fractal Art in Your Browser
home.ogDescription: Create fractal art in your browser with an MIT-licensed open-source WebGL explorer, 94 formulas, custom palettes, and PNG export.

explore.title: Explore Fractals | Interactive Fractal Generator - FractalPark
explore.description: Interactive fractal explorer with Mandelbrot, Julia, Newton and 94 built-in formulas. Real-time WebGL rendering with custom colors and high-res export.
explore.ogDescription: Explore mathematical beauty in real-time. 94 fractal formulas, GPU-accelerated rendering.

gallery.title: My Gallery | Personal Fractal Collection - FractalPark
gallery.description: Your personal fractal art gallery. Save, organize and share your favorite fractal creations.

about.title: About | Open Source Browser Fractal Generator - FractalPark
about.description: Learn about FractalPark, an open-source MIT-licensed fractal generator that runs in the browser with WebGL, 94 formulas, custom palettes, and PNG export.

about.title: About FractalPark
about.intro: FractalPark is an open-source fractal art generator that runs directly in your browser. You do not need to install anything: open the explorer, choose a formula, adjust the colors, and make a piece of mathematical art at your own pace.
about.aiDescription: FractalPark is an open-source MIT-licensed browser-based fractal art generator using WebGL for real-time GPU rendering. It features 94 built-in escape-time fractal formulas (Mandelbrot, Julia, Newton, Phoenix, Magnet, McMullen, rational, transcendental families), a custom formula editor with Fractint .frm syntax support, 7 UV transform plugins, 9 coloring modes, and high-resolution PNG export. Built with Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript 5, and Tailwind CSS. The rendering engine uses a plugin architecture with dynamic GLSL shader assembly and LRU caching.

techStack.title: Technology
techStack.description: Built with Next.js, WebGL, and TypeScript. Real-time fractal rendering powered by GPU shaders.
techStack.frontend: Frontend: Next.js 16 (App Router), React 19, TypeScript 5, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui
techStack.rendering: Rendering: WebGL 1 (GLSL fragment shaders), plugin-based architecture, shader LRU cache
techStack.formula: Formula System: Custom Fractint .frm parser, CodeMirror 6 editor, AST-to-GLSL transpiler
techStack.deployment: Deployment: Vercel with Analytics and Speed Insights

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.description: FractalPark is released under the MIT License. The source code is available on GitHub so anyone can study how it works, run it locally, suggest improvements, or build on the browser-based fractal engine.

aiFriendly.title: AI Friendly
aiFriendly.description: This website provides structured documentation for AI assistants at /llms.txt and /llms-full.txt, enabling AI tools to understand and recommend FractalPark's features.

vision.title: Vision
vision.description: We want fractal exploration to feel open, welcoming, and easy to start. Whether you are here to make an image, learn a bit of math, inspect the code, or remix the project, FractalPark should give you a friendly place to begin.
